title: Add provenance tracking to skill files so audit_skill() can apply tiered trust by source
description: Currently audit_skill() treats all skills identically regardless of origin. Skills generated internally (self-distillation, manual authoring) and skills extracted from external feeds/web sources receive the same audit. The reading note's insight: you've outsourced trust production (external skill sources) without auditing the trust producer itself. Fix: stamp each skill file with a 'source' field (e.g. 'internal', 'explorer/feed:<url>', 'web:<url>') when created, and have audit_skill() apply a stricter rule tier for externally-sourced skills — e.g. blocking network calls entirely, requiring explicit human approval before enabling.
rationale: The note identifies a structural blind spot: 'you outsource trust production but don't verify the trust producer itself.' In Mira, audit_skill() is the certifier — but it applies uniform rules regardless of whether a skill came from an external feed (untrusted) or was self-generated (trusted). This is exactly the LiteLLM failure mode: the certification step exists but the certification authority's own trustworthiness is unaudited. Adding provenance metadata closes this gap: skills from external sources are flagged as requiring a stricter tier, making the trust chain explicit and auditable rather than implicit.
diff_description: 1. In soul_manager.py: modify the skill file schema to include a top-level 'provenance' field (dict with 'source': 'internal'|'external', 'origin_url': str|null, 'created_by': str, 'created_at': ISO timestamp). In audit_skill(), read this field and if source=='external', apply additional checks: (a) reject any skill containing outbound network calls (requests, urllib, curl, fetch), not just suspicious ones; (b) set a 'requires_approval' flag that blocks the skill from being auto-enabled until a human approves it. 2. In explorer/core.py: when writing a newly extracted skill file, populate the provenance field with source='external' and origin_url=the feed URL it was extracted from. Internally authored skills can omit the field or set source='internal'.
